session_focus: User requested git track all AI memory since human does none of the work.
user_directives: User asked 'how can we make that change' to allow any LLM to be loaded.
User clarified 'ai is doing it all so there is no need to differentiate any of it'.
User emphasized 'i the user do none' - all work is AI-generated.
User wanted git to track everything without manual categorization.
assistant_responses: Checked .gitignore which only excludes vault credentials and security logs.
Ran git ls-files to verify all data/, vector_store/, iterations/, MEMORY.md, retrieval_log.jsonl are tracked.
Confirmed 16 session/miss JSONL, 47 vector entries, 31 iteration files all under version control.
Verified system already configured correctly - no changes needed.
training_value: When AI generates all work, git should track everything by default with minimal exclusions.
Only security-sensitive data (credentials, security audit logs) should be excluded.
Memory artifacts, training data, vector embeddings, iteration records are all valuable training signal.
The .gitignore should be minimal: only exclude what would compromise security if committed.
